description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player with a large, flared brass horn that dominates its upper section, featuring ornate floral embossing on the interior. The base is octagonal and crafted from dark wood, adorned with decorative golden floral motifs on each panel, giving it an antique and elegant appearance. A black turntable platter sits atop the base, with a visible vinyl record and a metallic spindle at its center. The tonearm is metallic, curved, and attached to the right side, with a crank handle on the left for manual operation. The overall construction combines wood, metal, and brass, with intricate detailing and a polished, slightly glossy finish. This object would be a striking decorative or functional piece in an urban vintage shop, park installation, or themed café.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden base, and visible turntable platter. Features intricate floral designs and a metallic tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide brass horn, ornate wooden base, and the tonearm positioned over the platter.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved tonearm, crank handle, and the depth of the horn's flare.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the circular platter, central spindle, and the detailed floral embossing inside the horn.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, tonearm, and the decorative panels on the wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying the central top of the wooden base. It appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with a visible vinyl record placed on it and a metallic spindle at the center. The platter is smooth, with no visible grooves or patterns, and is proportionally smaller than the horn but large enough to accommodate a standard record.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is medium in length, with a joint at the base for pivoting, and has a matte silver finish. The tonearm is positioned over the platter, suggesting it is ready to play a record.
